---
title: "Your Proposal Title Here"
---

<!-- DATA MANAGEMENT AND SHARING PLAN – 2 PAGE MAXIMUM (PAPPG II.D.2.i(ii))
     Uploaded under "Data Management and Sharing Plan" in the
     supplementary documentation section of Research.gov.
     Per NSF PAPPG, the plan must address:
       1. Types of data, samples, software, and other materials produced
       2. Standards for data and metadata format and content
       3. Policies for access and sharing, including privacy protections
       4. Policies for re-use, redistribution, and derivatives
       5. Plans for archiving and preservation of access -->

# Data Management and Sharing Plan

## Types of Data Produced

TODO: Describe the types of data, samples, physical collections,
software, models, and other materials to be produced in the course
of the project.

## Data Standards and Formats

TODO: Describe the standards and formats to be used for data and
metadata. Include file formats, documentation practices, and
codebook conventions.

## Access and Sharing

TODO: Describe how data will be shared during and after the project.
Include repositories (e.g., Harvard Dataverse, Zenodo, ICPSR),
access timelines, and any restrictions.

## Re-use and Redistribution

TODO: Describe policies for re-use, redistribution, and derivative
works. Address licensing (e.g., CC-BY, MIT) and any restrictions
on sensitive data.

## Preservation and Archiving

TODO: Describe the plan for long-term preservation of data and
access. Include the archive or repository, retention period, and
format migration strategy.
